summary: DHH enthusiastically reviews the second edition of 'Programming Ruby' (PickAxe II) by Dave Thomas, received at RubyConf. He highlights the book's comprehensive 800-page coverage including language introduction, unit testing, duck typing, RDoc, RubyGems, and complete references for built-in classes and standard library. He emphasizes that the reference material is original work by Dave Thomas, not recycled documentation. Despite being deeply experienced with Ruby, DHH says he gained at least ten new insights from just flipping through it.
key_points: The second edition of Programming Ruby is a massive 800-page comprehensive reference covering the language, techniques, tools, and complete class/method references
The reference and standard library sections are original work by Dave Thomas, unlike PHP books that reprint existing documentation
Even as a daily Ruby practitioner, DHH gained at least ten new important insights from a brief read-through
The Pragmatic Programmers gave free copies to the first 50 RubyConf attendees, generating enormous excitement
money_quotes: I had high expectations, but Dave Thomas and company has really blown those away.

Mind you, both the reference and the standard library is original work done mostly by Dave Thomas. This is not just a lame reprint of an already existing standard library documentation done by others.
I gained at least ten new important insights about that language and libraries in the few hours I spent flipping through. And I live and breathe Ruby for hours and hours each day.
Whether you're a Ruby veteran, neophyte, or just puzzled observer of all this Ruby raving. This is it. This is the book you want.
This is not just a lame reprint of an already existing standard library documentation done by others (which seems to be really in-vogue for PHP books to republish php.net).
key_insight: The second edition of Programming Ruby is a comprehensive, original reference work valuable even to experienced Ruby developers, setting a high bar for programming language books.
